h1: AI vs Human Document Processing: 2025 Benchmark Study

hero.headline: AI vs Human Document Processing: Complete 2025 Comparison
hero.subheadline: Detailed analysis of accuracy rates, processing speed, and total cost of ownership for automated vs manual document data extraction

summary: This comprehensive comparison examines AI automation versus human processing for extracting data from business documents. We analyze accuracy rates, processing speeds, cost structures, and practical limitations to help organizations choose the right approach for invoices, receipts, financial reports, and other structured documents.

faq.question: How accurate is AI document processing compared to humans?
faq.answer: AI achieves 99%+ accuracy on clear, standard documents like invoices and receipts. Human accuracy varies from 85-98% depending on fatigue, complexity, and experience level. However, humans handle unusual layouts and handwritten text better than current AI systems.

faq.question: What's the cost difference between AI and human document processing?
faq.answer: AI processing typically costs $0.10-0.50 per document through automation platforms. Manual processing costs $2-8 per document when factoring in wages, benefits, and overhead. The break-even point usually occurs around 50-100 documents monthly.

faq.question: How fast is AI vs human document processing?
faq.answer: AI processes standard invoices in 10-30 seconds per document. Experienced humans need 3-8 minutes per document for the same accuracy level. AI maintains consistent speed regardless of volume, while human speed decreases with fatigue.

faq.question: When should you choose human processing over AI?
faq.answer: Choose human processing for highly variable document formats, extensive handwritten content, complex judgment calls, or when processing fewer than 25 documents monthly. Humans excel at handling exceptions and unusual document structures that AI hasn't encountered.
